name: asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() in test files (deprecated loop pattern)
description: Test file uses asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() instead of pytest-asyncio. This pattern is deprecated in Python 3.10+ (DeprecationWarning) and raises RuntimeError in 3.12+ when called from a non-main thread or after the default loop is closed. LLMs frequently generate this pattern as a 'safe' fallback that worked in Python 3.6-3.9.

bad_example.why_bad: get_event_loop() is deprecated. In Python 3.12, calling it outside of a running loop raises DeprecationWarning or RuntimeError. The entire test file manually calls run_until_complete() instead of using @pytest.mark.asyncio + await, which also means tests share loop state and can leak coroutines.

good_example.code:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_creative_reasoning_score():
    result = await engine.evaluate_novelty(prompt)
    assert result["novelty_score"] == pytest.approx(0.5)

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_feasibility_score():
    result = await engine.evaluate_feasibility(prompt)
    assert result["feasibility_score"] == pytest.approx(0.5)
good_example.why_better: pytest-asyncio handles event loop lifecycle. pytest.approx() handles float comparisons correctly.

source: deep_think_mcp scan 2026-06-03; tests/test_creative_reasoning.py:206,214,222,230,243,267,285 (7 occurrences in one file)
